name: @radzor/http-client
description: Resilient HTTP client with automatic retry, exponential backoff, timeout, and request/response interceptors. Built on the native fetch API. No dependencies.

inputs.name: baseUrl
inputs.description: Base URL prepended to all requests (e.g. https://api.example.com).

inputs.name: retries
inputs.description: Maximum number of retry attempts on transient failures (5xx, network errors).

inputs.name: retryDelay
inputs.description: Initial retry delay in milliseconds. Doubles with each attempt (exponential backoff).

inputs.name: headers
inputs.description: Default headers added to every request (e.g. Authorization, Accept).

outputs.name: response
outputs.type: { status: number; headers: Record<string, string>; data: unknown }
outputs.description: Parsed HTTP response with status, headers, and JSON-decoded body.

events.name: onRetry
events.description: Fired when a request is retried.

events.name: onError
events.description: Fired when all retry attempts are exhausted.

actions.name: post
actions.description: Perform a POST request with a JSON body.

actions.name: put
actions.description: Perform a PUT request with a JSON body.

actions.name: request

params.name: options
params.description: Body, headers, timeout, and other options.

actions.returns: Promise<{ status: number; headers: Record<string, string>; data: unknown }>
actions.description: Generic request method for any HTTP verb.

llm.constraints: Retries only on 5xx responses and network errors — not on 4xx. Uses native fetch (Node.js 18+ or browser). For streaming responses, use request() with a custom handler.
